items.correct: The process by which wealthier residents move into a previously low-income urban area, raising property prices and often displacing original residents.
items.misconception: Gentrification is when the government rebuilds an old part of the city.
items.random_wrong: Gentrification means the city becomes more polluted as it grows.
items.why: Students confuse gentrification (private market-led displacement) with regeneration (planned redevelopment). The key feature is who benefits and who is pushed out.

items.q: Why can a cold winter NOT disprove climate change?
items.correct: Climate is the long-term statistical average; a single cold winter is a weather event. Climate change is the trend in averages and extremes over decades, not the absence of cold days.
items.misconception: A really cold winter proves global warming is not happening.
items.random_wrong: Climate change only happens in summer.
items.why: This is one of the most common public misconceptions. Climate is the slope of the trend over 30+ years; individual weather events are scatter around that line — both can be cold.

items.q: What is the difference between the focus and epicentre of an earthquake?
items.correct: The focus (hypocentre) is the underground point where the earthquake originates; the epicentre is the point on the surface directly above the focus.
items.misconception: The focus and epicentre are the same point.
items.random_wrong: The focus is at the centre of the Earth and the epicentre is on the surface.
items.why: Students treat the two terms as synonymous or place the focus deep at the Earth's core. The focus is just below the epicentre, typically only a few to tens of kilometres deep.

items.q: Which African region suffers severe desertification?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Sahel. “Maghreb” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: What is the maximum rainfall in hot deserts?
items.why: Correct answer: 250mm. “100mm” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: What percentage of Northern Hemisphere is permafrost?
items.why: Exact answer: 25 %. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
